title: Go 1.26 and AWS SDK for Go v2 Client Contract · Execution Trace: Operation Options Scope
summary: A service copies an SDK v1 tutorial, floats module versions, shares request options incorrectly, and treats generated Go types as runtime validation. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the operation options scope increment in pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply per-operation functional options without mutating shared client configuration or leaking tenant-specific endpoints.
Use SDK pointers, enums, document types, output metadata, paginators, and nil handling without losing API semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Operation Options Scope · Theory Operation Options Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3616 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Operation Options Scope contract for the supplied data.
